pages.title: Gem Coatings
pages.text: A coated part retains both materials.$(p)The visible gem contributes its trait, and the metal core contributes its own trait. For example, a diamond coating over a gold core supplies both Adamant and Gilded.$(p)The same material always supplies the same trait in every eligible component slot.

pages.title: Diamond and Emerald
pages.text: $(bold)Adamant — diamond$()$(li)×1.75 suitable-block mining speed$(li)Attacks bypass 30% of armour mitigation$(li)No condition or wear penalty$(p)$(bold)Fortunate — emerald$()$(li)Fortune and Looting gain 2 effective levels$(li)Works from level zero$(li)May exceed the enchantment's normal maximum$(p)Diamond owns general mining speed; emerald owns resource yield.

pages.title: Amethyst and Sapphire
pages.text: $(bold)Resonant — amethyst$()$(li)The strongest installed non-curse enchantment gains 2 effective levels$(p)$(bold)Focused — sapphire$()$(li)×1.15 mining speed$(li)+0.55 attack speed$(li)Ignores airborne and underwater mining penalties$(li)Crossbows gain effective Quick Charge II$(p)Repeated components use the normal diminishing trait scaling.

pages.title: Ruby and Topaz
pages.text: $(bold)Keen — ruby$()$(li)×1.15 suitable-block mining speed$(li)×1.35 physical damage$(li)Reliable combat material$(p)$(bold)Forceful — topaz$()$(li)×1.30 suitable-block mining speed$(li)×1.60 physical damage$(li)×1.50 durability wear$(p)Ruby and topaz remain usable tool heads, but neither outruns diamond at general mining.

pages.title: Flint
pages.text: $(bold)Jagged — flint$()$(li)×1.20 suitable-block mining speed$(li)×1.35 physical damage$(li)×2 durability wear$(p)Jagged is an accessible early hybrid: useful output, deliberately poor longevity, and no claim on diamond's late-game mining role.
